zh.lifeSummary: William Clark於大約1620年出生在英國。他於大約1645年與Katherine Bunce在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1681年7月22日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈達姆享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Clark于大约1620年出生在英国。他于大约1645年与Katherine Bunce在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1681年7月22日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈達姆享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clerk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Jacques.
Americanized form of French   Clerc   and perhaps also of the Dutch and Flemish cognates   De Klerk   and   De Clerck   or of variants of these names. Compare   Clark   3.

variants.name: Clerkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Brendan, Dympna, Seamus.

Irish (Monaghan): from an Irish pet form of English clerk ‘cleric’ (Irish cléireach), see Clark . Alternatively, an Anglicized form of Ó Cléireacháin, ‘descendant of little cleric’, an Irish diminutive of the word. See also McClurkin , an Antrim surname from Mac Cléirchín ‘son of little cleric’.

variants.name: Calarco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Carmelo, Fausto, Nunziato, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): probably a nickname derived from Greek kalē archē ‘good beginning’. Compare Clark 4.

variants.name: Clerc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Odette, Sebastien, Serge.

French: occupational or status name for a member of a minor religious order or for a scholar, Old French clerc, from Late Latin clericus, from Greek klērikos, a derivative of klēros ‘inheritance, legacy’, with reference to the priestly tribe of Levites (see Levy ) ‘whose inheritance was the Lord’. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established. In the Middle Ages it was virtually only members of religious orders who learned to read and write, so that the term clerc later came to denote any literate man, a scholar (hence its modern meaning ‘scribe, secretary’ or ‘clerk’). Compare Leclerc .

variants.name: Clarkson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from   Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: William Clark was born about 1620, in England. He married Katherine Bunce about 1645, in Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 22 July 1681, in Haddam, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 62.

Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 22 juillet 1681, à Haddam, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 62 ans.

it.lifeSummary: William Clark è nato il circa 1620 nel England. Lui sposato Katherine Bunce circa 1645, nel Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 22 luglio 1681, nel Haddam, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Clarkは約1620年にイギリスで生まれた。約1645年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州でKatherine Bunceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1681年7月22日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハッドアムで62歳で死去した。

ko.Middletown, Hartford, Connecticut Colony: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 맷압세트 미들레타운

ko.lifeSummary: William Clark(은)는 대략 1620년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1645년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주에서 Katherine Bunce(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1681년 7월 22일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하다에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Clark nasceu aproximadamente 1620, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Katherine Bunce aproximadamente 1645, em Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de julho de 1681, em Haddam, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William Clark родился около 1620 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Bunce около 1645 г. в местности Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 22 июля 1681 г. в местности хаддам, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William Clark nació aproximadamente 1620, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Katherine Bunce aproximadamente 1645, en Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 22 de julio de 1681, en Haddam, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 62 años.
